Subject: Andrew Farkas


There is $10mm left from this round. It was to have gone to lehman, but dick fuld was counseled, since the firm was a material investor, that he and the execs could not invest directly. This piece would go to abu dhabi, but you can have it if you want. You were here first and you have been unfailingly supportive. Just let me know.

subject: Re: Andrew Farkas
body:
how many floors in chyrsler

----- Original Message ----
From: Cecilia Steen <cecilia.steen@gmail.com>
To: JE <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, October 31, 2006 9:34:38 PM
Subject: Andrew Farkas






Pls convey this to Jeffery.  I am told that these 3 choices are amongst the coolest spaces in the city right now.  Tell him that I will arrange tours if wishes to see them.  He needs to move quickly though as these things fly off the shelf.


 






From: Siegel, Stephen @ New York [mailto:
Stephen.Siegel@cbre.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, October 31, 2006 5:03 PM

To: Farkas, Andrew
Subject: FW: From Jeffrey Epstein


 

for epstein

 





From:
 Wildes, Alan @ New York 
Sent: Monday, October 30, 2006 5:14 PM
To: Siegel, Stephen @ New York

Subject: RE: From Jeffrey Epstein

for starters:

1)  the entire 71st floor of the chrysler bldg. 4,164 sf @ $120 psf.   not for a heavy density user though.  space will max out w/4-5 offices/conf rooms + support.  portion of the space has 18' ceilings.


2)  part 11th floor @ 667 Madison.  5,765 rsf @ $150 psf.  space built out beautifully and has 2 terraces.  no real views to speak of (sliver of the park) but good light.  in my opinion, the classiest bldg in manhattan for a smaller user.



3)  the entire 18th floor @ 767 5th (GM).  balance of the floor is mechanical.  space has 18' ceilings.  deal just died on it. maybe too big though @ 8,800 rsf ($120 a foot) but park views and a full floor at that size is a real find.




 


Alan Wildes
 | Senior Vice President

CB Richard Ellis 
| Global Corporate Services (Brokerage)


200 Park Avenue
,19th Floor | New York, New York  10166
T 212 984 8313 | F 212 984 6699



alan.wildes@cbre.com | 
www.cbre.com





 


 





From:
 Siegel, Stephen @ New York 
Sent: Monday, October 30, 2006 4:19 PM
To: Wildes, Alan @ New York

Subject: FW: From Jeffrey Epstein

do you have anything?

 





From:
 Farkas, Andrew [mailto:afarkas@islecap.com] 
Sent: Monday, October 30, 2006 10:02 AM

To: Siegel, Stephen @ New York
Subject: FW: From Jeffrey Epstein


See the attached.  Price is no object, but he needs 4k-5k of super high class, high impact, awesome and unusual space.  Any ideas?

cecilia.steen@gmail.com] 
Sent: Monday, October 30, 2006 9:44 AM

Subject: From Jeffrey Epstein



Andrew,



Jeffrey said he needs 4000 spectucular sqft of office space starting in summer.



Cecilia

About 5 or 6 weeks ago I sent jeffery a book about
yacht haven grande
("yhg") food and beverage. As he is one of the few
original investors in yhg
who did not convert into island global yachting
("igy") stock, he has an
opportunity to take a pro rata position in the
enterprise that will be
owning and operating all of the restaurtants at yhg.
Anything that is not
subscribed by outside parties (effectively him and 2
other small investors)
will be subscribed by igy. Since he already has an
investment in igy, it
might be redundant for him to make a $1.2mm
investment in the f+b
operations, but I like to do things right and thus I
need a yes or no from
him. If he says no (probably the right decision for
him because it's just so
small) he will still own some of it through his igy
holdings. I just need an
answer. Thanks.

--- Cecilia Steen cecilia.steen@gmail.com wrote:

There is a guy in puerto rico who was in the refuse
business named Filiberto
Lebron. He owns the land inland from pier 10 in old
san juan harbor. I am
told that he is butt buddies with the gov. He wants
to see me abt the
development of piers 6-10 (remember that we are
participating in an rfp to
develop pier 6 and are hoping to extend it to at
least pier 8). He told one
of the guys at banco popular that he has been in
contact with wayne huizenga
(to whom he sold his business) about doing a jv on
this same property with
him, but, according to the BP guy, "he would rather
do it with us." Who can
blame him? So, since you are my rabbi in puerto
rico, here's what I need to
know: with the sponsorship of both bp and you in our
arsenal, do I need to
kiss this guy's hairy rump (Filiberto) to ensure
success even if he is an
asshole or do you expect that we can be fine even if
we are without the
benefits of the radiance of his glow accompanying
our bid? In other words,
do I need to let him in to take him out under almost
any circumstance or, if
he becomes unreasonable, can I tell him to pound
sand knowing that the ceo
of bp and uncle jeff will save me from the bully in
the school yard? If this
question is unclear, then please give me a call. I
need to be very careful
with the politics of this.

I hear you were unwell. You must be very careful
where you put your mouth
these days. I am glad to hear that you are
recovering.

Sent from my BlackBerry Wireless Handheld
